dialog.stepLabel: Chat with the students by the auditorium door

items.speaker: Shy Fan
items.text: Oh, hello… are you here for the Fleet Snowfluff fan meeting? The venue is still being set up, but once it's ready, please join.

items.speaker: Outgoing Fan
items.text: She's a singer! A campus legend. She's only released online, but she's super popular.

items.speaker: Shy Fan
items.text: But… her site hasn't been updated in over ten years. She posted one last demo and then… nothing.

items.speaker: Outgoing Fan
items.text: Don't be such a downer. Maybe she just graduated and moved on to bigger things.

items.speaker: Outgoing Fan
items.text: Probably never showed her face to keep her life private. Doesn't matter, we still have her music!

items.speaker: Outgoing Fan
items.text: Here! Even if you're not a fan, you should give her a listen. I'm new here, but even I was hooked when I first heard her songs!

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: The outgoing fan shoves a poster into your hands.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Hm, nice print.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: A fan meeting... huh? If they're happy, I'm happy.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: C'mon, let's follow the Soliskin and see what's going on in the auditorium!

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: What?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Oh, just a habit of mine. Waving, chatting, going to class... it makes me feel like I'm still alive.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: If I stop, I might forget what it felt like to be human. Call it… a self-reminder.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: ...Fleet Snowfluff?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Yep, that's me. Ugh, but don't say my alias out loud! It's embarrassing—

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: So you weren't just a Synchronist. You had a whole other life here.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Just one of my many hobbies. I was into photography, gaming, movie nights with friends…

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Figured you only get to be a student for a few years. And I wasn't the ambitious researcher type. I wanted to soak it all up while I could.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I tried a little bit of everything. I just wanted a happy, carefree life!

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Who knew I'd end up as a ghost, stuck as a student for ten more years… How awful is that? Wait... does this mean I'm never gonna graduate?!

options.text: You might get the chance once you restore your body.
I wish I could be a student forever!

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Hope so. Guess everyone needs something to hold onto. It's good to have a little hope.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Haha! I get it. School life is easier. A whole lot less complicated.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: But I have to say... It's really moving to hear people talk about my songs after all this time.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I wrote those for myself, you know? Never expected so many people to care.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: But... if they made someone feel something—joy, pain, anything—that makes me really happy!

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Hey, listen. Hear the song playing in the auditorium? That's the unfinished demo I left on my site.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: What a shame. I really should've finished that before the training run. Now it's my little musical... epitaph. Haha.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Let's go! The Soliskin are calling us.

items.speaker: Past Surprised Student
items.text: Is that the student rep from Rabelle College? That's one bold speech…

items.speaker: Past Mocking Student
items.text: Haha, is she giving a welcome speech or recruiting for a space adventure club? Uh-oh, the professors look upset.

items.speaker: Past Gentle Student
items.text: But she made a good point, didn't she? Isn't that passion the entire reason we're here? To explore whatever's out there again?

items.speaker: Past Impressed Teacher
items.text: Aemeath, right? I remember her sync test scores were off the charts. We might be looking at this year's prime Exostrider candidate.

items.speaker: Past Stern Teacher
items.text: Don't be so sure. Heard about her extracurriculars? Put nicely, she's quite the dabbler. Put bluntly, she doesn't take anything seriously.

items.speaker: Past Thoughtful Teacher
items.text: Potential is still potential. She's brilliant. She just needs… guidance.

items.speaker: Past Aemeath
items.text: —May we one day walk among the stars, to touch with our own hands a universe so cold, so real, and so breathtakingly beautiful.

items.speaker: Past Aemeath
items.text: That's all from me. If I keep going, my credits this year might be in danger!

items.speaker: Past Aemeath
items.text: It's an amazing feeling to be here at Startorch Academy, and to know I'll be sharing this journey into the great unknown with all of you. As a student, I can't wait to start this new chapter of my life.

items.speaker: Past Aemeath
items.text: There's just one thing missing today... I wish my family could be here in the crowd to hear this.

items.speaker: Past Aemeath
items.text: So, to {Male=him;Female=her}, I want to say: I'm doing great. And I'm happy. Everything is just as you hoped it would be. So please, don't worry about me.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Looking back… I really do miss those days.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Startorch Academy is a nice place. I caused my fair share of trouble, but the professors never turned me away from lectures.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You saved Lahai-Roi, Aemeath. But you paid for it with your future, and no one even knows...

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Maybe a little? But it's fine. Loneliness doesn't kill. Besides, I still have plenty to keep myself busy with!

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Of course not. If I hadn't have done what I did, so many people would have died. Besides, saving the world isn't a lousy feeling!

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I just... I wish I could've been there. At graduation. To hug my friends and wish them all the happiness in the world.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: The Soliskin headed outside! Let's catch up!

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: If it's leaving the campus, it's probably going to the frostlands… Right, we'll need to take the Starward Riseway.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: That place above the sword?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Yes. A frozen, silent plain, white with endless snow… the place where the Exostrider finally sank into slumber.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (My frequency loss… It's getting worse!)

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Aaaand... nope! Still on the fritz. How are you feeling?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I'm… okay. After that mech flew off, I felt less of my frequency bleeding out.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (That mech's frequency felt like the presence from the rift back in Honami City.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Aemeath, that mech only left after it saw you. After you clashed with it.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: That's twice you've saved me. Each time I'm left with more questions than answers. They say you're the Exostrider's Synchronist who's been missing for over ten years.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: And your hand back there...

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Me? I'm not the Exostrider's Synchronist now.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I'm its Resonator.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I just... died first. Call me a digital ghost!

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Ten plus years back, the underground seal broke. The Threnodian on the other side of the Stridergate was about to swallow Lahai-Roi.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I was still a student. My sync with the Exostrider hadn't hit a hundred percent yet.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: But there was no other choice. The Exostrider was still sleeping, so I had to do something... unorthodox.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: So you Overclocked yourself to try to resonate with it?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Yup. Impressive, right? I woke the big guy up, if only for a second.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: The seal held. The crisis averted. Everyone I cared about... they made it out okay.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: But it was too much for me. My body couldn't take it and got totally torn apart.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: So yeah. I died. But I'm still here... just, floatin' around. I've been a little invisible. Well, a lot invisible. To everyone until you.

options.text: Why am I the only one that can see you?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: You can see me now because of this little guy.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: The Exostrider's Reactor Core. My existence is kinda tethered to it now. Think of it as my heart.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Even as a digital ghost, I never stopped trying to pull my "self" back together. To get back to reality.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: There's something I gotta do. And I gotta be the Exostrider's true Resonator to see it through.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: But our Resonance started with an Overclock. The power I got was unstable from the get-go.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I've spent years building it back up with this Core. The Soliskin have been my little helpers, too.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Then, not long ago, you ended up with it. That became your backstage pass to seeing me.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: "Not long ago"... You mean when the Reactor Drive fell?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Bingo! Since then, the Core has become energy stored inside you. This is the first time I managed to make it solid. Took me a bit to figure out the trick.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Then it was you. You're the one who saved Mornye when she was dying.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: You and that junior of yours were always close. We even crossed paths a few times back when I was still... well, around. Of course I'd help.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (A junior of mine? So I really did know Mornye in the past...)

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Oh, and by the way? When you charged in to save Professor Mornye you looked pretty cool!

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Alright, no more teasing. But I mean it. No matter what, you always choose to save.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: And to me... that's the coolest thing in the world. The most beautiful.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Anyway! Back to business. The Reactor Core is tied to Sigillum—that's the mech that attacked you.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: It's like those Voidworms you fought. Mostly made of Exostrider parts, but corrupted. Contaminated by Threnodian Voidmatter.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Sigillum... stole a chunk of my power. That threw a wrench in my whole "reassembling myself" project. Now it's after the Core, too.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Since Sigillum holds this piece of the Exostrider's power, it can't be killed completely as long as the Exostrider and I exist. Now, it can only be me who stops it.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: And since just touching the thing makes your frequency drain, taking it down should plug your leak, too.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Plus, I get my power back. Win-win.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: But see what it's doing to me? Even with you holding the Core, I keep flickering in and out. One second I can touch you, the next I'm fading right through. So annoying—

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: So will you help? You've got the Core, and the Reactor Drive is wrecked. I can't do this alone.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Also, I've noticed... when the Core is in your hand, it runs faster.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I can tell you're leaving a few things out, Aemeath.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Still, you helped me and a lot of people in Lahai-Roi. That's enough for me to trust you. What do you need me to do?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: "Existence" is the imprint life leaves behind. Being remembered, talked about, and seen...

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: All the places I went when I was alive bear those imprints. The Core's running lets me find and record those frequencies.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: These little cuties have been my scouts, and they'll be our guides now, too.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Pretty easy, huh? All you gotta do is carry the Core and follow our lead.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I'll do it.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Alrighty! Thanks in advance, study buddy.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Cold?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I'm fine. What about you?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Between you and me, ghosts can't feel the cold.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: The snow... It's so beautiful.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: What a coincidence to run into you here, {PlayerName}. This is a pleasant surprise.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Oh… it's Dr. Luuk. When he's around, just pretend I'm not here. Otherwise, you'll look like a weirdo talking to thin air.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: You should hide too, okay? This is our secret journey.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You know him?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: He's the school doctor. All the students know him. I didn't super well, but I do know that he's been in Lahai-Roi for a long time, and… you can trust him.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: You came to the frostlands for the Exostrider?

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: I'm headed the same way. I've got to check in on some researchers stationed out here on long-term fieldwork.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Having a guide might make the trip a little easier. What do you say?

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Let me help you modify your bike. There are quite a few waterways, and its current setup might not handle them well.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You know how to modify bikes?

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: You pick up a few things when you come here as often as I do.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Oh, I used to love riding! My friends even called me the Speed Queen! Though that was usually while they were white-knuckling the seat and screaming. Heh.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Look at this place, {PlayerName}. Hard to believe it, but years ago, all of this was a lush, green forest.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Back then, the Roya were nomads, the true natives of Lahai-Roi, who walked in step with nature itself.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Was it the Lament that changed it into frostlands?

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Yes. It triggered a magnetic pole shift. From then on, Lahai-Roi became the new pole.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: But the Lament wasn't the only cataclysm stirring back then.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: In the past, we had already reached the stars. But at some point… the <te href=750035>Etheric Sea</te> spread to cover all of Solaris.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: From that moment on...

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: From that moment on, the universe locked its doors. We couldn't verify whether the sky above us was real or just a projection.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Countless spacecraft were lost. Our space exploration programs were abandoned. And space became out of reach.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: But times have changed. Since its founding, the goal of Startorch Academy has been to use the Exostrider to explore space—whether through the Etheric Sea or the <te href=750036>Stridergate</te>.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I understand the Etheric Sea part, but the Stridergate…?

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: You know Lahai-Roi's history. Both the Exostrider and the Threnodian first came to Solaris through the Stridergate.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: The Academy has its theories, but here are two you might find interesting: <color=YellowD>the Stridergate is a gateway to outer space, and Aleph-1 is a Threnodian alien to Solaris</color>.

options.text: An outer space gateway you say…

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: With a gateway like that, of course we want to open it. But to pass through, we must awaken the Exostrider and be prepared to face Aleph-1 head-on.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: I've long believed that, Aleph-1 poses a far greater threat beyond Void Storms and Voidworms。

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: With other Threnodians, we can at least glean clues of their nature from their places of origin and the civilizations they emerge from. But Aleph-1… our knowledge remains blank.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: So many dream of being the voyager who returns from the stars, the first to walk the cosmos once more. But in all these years, no one has succeeded.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: The <color=RedD>Etheric Sea Phenomenon</color>… cast us into isolation. But it also gave birth to all our dreams of what could lie beyond.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: And those dreams… are still alive today.

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: Hm? Y-you are the one who was talking to yourself in class…

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Whoopsies...

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: Oh, and Dr. Herssen! You are... traveling together? Did the Academy greenlight a new field trip?

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Just my routine checks on the field researchers. {PlayerName} here has their own business to attend to, but we're headed the same way.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: What about you, Ms. Nivora? Chasing more fallen debris?

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: Yes! Exactly. I-I heard the frostlands have been experiencing changes since Helios was launched… I had a hypothesis that new debris fields might have been exposed!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Debris?

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: Space equipment that fell from the sky long ago! Probes, satellites, and even space stations…!

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: Some previous digs unearthed wreckage containing records lost during the Lament.

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: Oh, and images! Photos taken in orbit of other planets! They were absolutely… breathtakingly beautiful!!

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: Huff... Huff. Oh, sorry, I-I got carried away...

options.text: It's okay. Passion is a good thing.
Hey, I'm into those things too.

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: Thank you… Sometimes, I'll just start info-dumping... I know it's not the best…

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: Really?! That's great! I'll... I'll show you the data I've collected next time we're in class!

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: The universe… What secrets is it hiding beyond all those infinite stars?

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: I really want to know.

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: Anyway, in short, I came here for the fallen debris.

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: The data on the instrument shows there's a high chance that close by… Eh?

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Run!

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: I knew it! There had to have been undiscovered debris in this area!

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: It must've been sealed in the ice wall all this time…

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Luuk, Nivora. You alright?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Oh, I'm good! Perks of being a ghost, y'know.

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: Ah! I'm so sorry... It's my fault for putting everyone in danger...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The collapse was an accident. We're all okay, so don't blame yourself.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: But the way we came in is completely blocked.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Scaling that ice wall… It's too high, too risky. I'd rather not have a student on that climb.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: I've contacted the Academy. They're sending a rescue team. Once Nivora's safely returned, we'll set out again.

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: Mmm...

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Hey, little guy here says our next stop is deep in the ice. There's a path at the bottom of the station. Wanna check it out?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: What about waiting inside the station? It's freezing out here, and another collapse is possible. It's safer to wait for the rescue team indoors.

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: I-I agree! I want to see what's inside too...

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: With {PlayerName} on the team, a quick recon should be manageable. Up to you.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Hey, question. Before you came to Lahai-Roi, did you do this kind of thing a lot?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: What do you mean?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Like… traveling with a crew, seeing the sights, stumbling into trouble, then going your separate ways.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Like a classic road trip movie, you know?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Nice. I happen to love those.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Oh, so... more big-budget action? I can see that. Solaris is crazy.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Looks like this time I get a co-starring role. Amazing. Let's keep going, study buddy.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Was that... me in that recording?)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (That had to have been the station's final moments, when the "<te href=750035>Etheric Sea</te> Phenomenon" suddenly spread and engulfed all of Solaris, like Luuk said.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (But those droplets in the sky… that was Retroact Rain. So that might've been an image of an event long ago, not part of the Etheric Sea Phenomenon itself.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Did I really fall from the Etheric Sea? Or what lay beyond it? Junxi did say the droplets could act as some kind of gateway.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Back then, could I have...)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Aemeath?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: ...Hm? What's up?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Haha! Not at all.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I'm fine, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: I'd recommend you keep this private. Don't leave the recording for the Academy's teams to find.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Remember what I said earlier? It's a nest of competing factions. Anything connected to your past needs to be handled with discretion.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: So, what do you think? Leader... of the Black Shores.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (...Very few people know about that.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Be direct with me, Luuk.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I know you knew me before. But I acted mostly as a Bloom Bearer in the past.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You and I, what exactly are we to each other?

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Mm... friends, I'd say. Not the kind who lived in each other's pockets, but... as a Huanglong saying puts it, "True friendships are still waters that run deep."

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: We met by chance, and by another twist of fate, you entrusted me with a rather long-term assignment.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: How about this: "Strangers in passing who became confidants." How does that definition suit you?

options.text: I need time before I can take your word for it.
Fine... but more vague than I'd like.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: That's fine. Your work in Lahai-Roi won't be finished so soon, and we still have plenty of opportunities to reacquaint ourselves.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Heh, sorry, but it's the truth. We didn't spend much time together, but we got along instinctively.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Startorch is a web of competing interests. If this video leaks, it'd create quite the cleanup operation.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: After your sudden exit years ago, people were lining up to unpack your history. Solaris's golden boy, forever a mystery.

options.text: Thank you for handling the cleanup.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Heh... You're not the same as when we first met. But let's save the reminiscing for later.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Nivora's hurt. I've stabilized her, but as a doctor, I must stay to watch over her.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: From this point, you'll have to proceed alone. We'll wait here for the Academy's retrieval team.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Alright. I'll see you back at the infirmary.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Luuk, when you said I'm "not the same," what did you mean?

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Just my impression, I could be wrong. But you seem... lighter now.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: More hopeful. Happier.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: And that's... not a bad thing, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: Now, we reach the second epoch of the Royan Trine Myth, the Sacred Reactor Epoch.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: After Baldur, the Sun's incarnation, had sealed the foreign divinity Hvedrungr, the frostlands fell into an endless dream… Assistant Nivora, would you continue?

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: O-okay. T-this section...  it describes how the Exostrider used its greatsword to seal the Threnodian behind the <te href=750036>Stridergate</te>.

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: The records say both the Exostrider and the Threnodian travelled through the Stridergate to reach Lahai-Roi. Their battle was fierce.

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: The Threnodian pushed the Exostrider to the brink of defeat.

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: But at that moment, for reasons unknown, the Exostrider's Reactor Drive flared with blinding light—
—And it found the strength to raise its sword and seal the Threnodian away. Lahai-Roi was saved. What a relief!

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Hi, nice to meet you. I'm Aemeath from Rabelle College, same as you.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: First time seeing you in class. Trying to collect credits from all four departments? That's ambitious. Welcome to the academic quagmire.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Aemeath?! I've been looking for you.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I never got the chance to thank you.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Thank me?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The day the Eye of the Threnodian appeared, you drove it back and saved me.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Did I? …Sorry, I don't remember that.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (She doesn't sound like she's lying. Then how...)

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: A Synchronist… {PlayerName}… Hahaha!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: What's so funny?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Oh, don't mind me. I'm just not used to seeing you like this.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I mean, look at you. Something's missing, though... Oh, I know, a uniform!

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: The Academy still hasn't issued you one? They're painfully slow sometimes.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Aemeath?

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: I have to go.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Gotta run, Professor!

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: {PlayerName}, let's try to channel that enthusiasm into listening. Do come see me in my office after class, okay?

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: Assistant Nivora, if you would? Pick up from the Twilight Epoch's Unending Winter, please.

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: Sigrika, could you come to the library with me?

items.speaker: Sigrika
items.text: Okay. Hey... Did you notice the new student? The one who kept talking to {Male=himself;Female=herself} during class.

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: Hmm—

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: {PlayerName}. Something happened during class, didn't it?

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: …So a girl named Aemeath just got up and left the lecture hall?

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: Heh. I never expected to hear that name again. Certainly not like this.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: Perhaps you've just had a run-in with a "ghost"?

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: After all... You were the only one who saw her.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: President Lucilla, what do you know about her?

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: Stay at Rabelle College long enough, and you'll eventually hear her name mentioned. Though it's a story from over a decade ago now.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: Aemeath was a Synchronist. Brilliant. Popular. Professors had high hopes for her.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: Then, during a piloting drill, she simply vanished.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: She climbed into a simulator with the others. But when the cockpit reopened, hers was empty.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: Intrigued? Want to go find her?

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: Of course. You're exactly the type to get pulled into Aemeath's story.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: She saved you…? Heh, seems the mysteries around that girl are still multiplying.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: To see someone who vanished years ago, presumed dead, lingering around the Academy… A figment of your imagination, or is there more to it? I'm very curious to find out.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: She disappeared from the Synchrolink Training Grounds. The very cockpit she vanished from is still there.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: The Academy turned it inside out at the time and found nothing. But if you're interested, go take a look.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: I expect you'll dig up some new surprises for us.

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: ...Huh?

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: Ugh! That hurts...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Nivora!

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Voidmatter saturation is critical! The SkyArk System is going into failure... I'm going in to try to stabilize it!

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Everything here has already been contaminated, including... it.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: From a doctor's perspective, the only healing left for what this man has become... is release.

items.speaker: Nivora
items.text: There's a collapsed entry point over there, but… it's blocked. Some kind of barrier caused by unstable frequencies.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: The Exostrider sleeps in these frostlands. Void Storm activity can make local frequencies unstable with <te href=750037>fluctuating Voidmatter levels</te>.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Oh, taking this opportunity, {PlayerName}, let me grant you access to the <te href=750038>Module Adapter</te>… Hm? It's already unlocked.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Hm? That must've been Lucilla.)

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Let's move to an area with a stronger signal so it can be deployed.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Aemeath definitely glossed over some things.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Like what happened during our previous encounter, what she's done since becoming a digital ghost, and the real reason why Sigillum stole her power.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (After years drifting like this, to still hold onto that spirit... Her will must be strong. Prying answers from her won't be easy.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (I'll bide my time. The right moment will come.)

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Has something happened, Mentor?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I see. So the Reactor Drive's Core survived the crash?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: It must be the key to reactivating the Exostrider. Of course, then the official stance is that it be returned. Startorch Academy and the Roya would both agree on that.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: But, as you said, curiosity from the Institute is inevitable—

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Mornye?

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: My, my. Already caused a stir in the training grounds? Color me impressed.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: I'll have people clean up the aftermath. You can carry on as you see fit.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: …You were monitoring my comms with Mornye.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: You're a person of special interest. Of course we keep an eye on your movements.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: "We"?

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: Yes, "we." The Spacetrek Collective. Think of us as the annoying adults on campus.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: I'll handle the debate between the Institute and the Academy about the Core. This is a matter of priority. They'll listen to me.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: But when you're done with your business, you will return the Core to the Exostrider, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: You didn't mention it to Mornye, but I suspect... your possession of it traces back to Aemeath, doesn't it?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: President Lucilla, you're asking a lot of questions. And I don't appreciate being interrogated over a tapped line.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: Heh, my apologies. As the one responsible for this Academy, matters related to the Exostrider tend to cloud my judgement.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: After the incident with Warren, we upgraded safety protocols on public lines. No more overstepping.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: Here's a peace offering. I've granted you full clearance: passes for the Starward Riseway, archive access, and use of Academy weaponry and equipment.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: I'll also personally apologize to Professor Mornye for interrupting your comms and ensure her next research grant is... substantially approved.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: How's that, {PlayerName}? More to your liking?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: …Why is the Spacetrek Collective pushing this so hard?

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: You should know. You have a past in Lahai-Roi, and you have some kind of connection with the Exostrider.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: The Twilight Epoch, the Unending Winter described in Royan legend... the threat of the Threnodian Aleph-1 is growing. We need the Exostrider awakened. Returning that Reactor Core is the only way.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: We've always trusted you to stand with us in this matter. To stand on the side of humanity.

items.speaker: Lucilla
items.text: You know where to find me if you need anything, {PlayerName}. And my door is always open if you just want to talk.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: This Module Adapter was developed by Startorch Academy for frostland expeditions. It lets you interact with frequencies disrupted by Voidmatter.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Give it a try. You'll need it here.
